1.q: The ORF of AoD9D1 and AoD9D2, encoding a protein of 456 and 469 amino acids (aa), respectively, was cloned... AoD9D1 and AoD9D2 obviously belong to two groups, and both groups quite closely with D9D in Aspergillus oryzae RIB40.

2.q: the overexpression of AozC led to a downregulation of the FAD2 gene, a pivotal enzyme in the biosynthesis of unsaturated fatty acids (UFAs)

3.q: the contents of unsaturated fatty acids, including palmitoleic acid and oleic acid, in the AoD9D-overexpressing S. cerevisiae were significantly higher

4.q: the contents of unsaturated fatty acids, including palmitoleic acid and oleic acid, in the AoD9D-overexpressing S. cerevisiae were significantly higher

5.q: the overexpression of AozC led to a downregulation of the FAD2 gene, a pivotal enzyme in the biosynthesis of unsaturated fatty acids (UFAs).

6.q: Transcriptome profiling further exposed the influence of AozC on the regulation of UFA biosynthesis... AozC functions as a negative regulator of salt tolerance in A. oryzae.

7.q: the expression of the FAD2 gene, implicated in fatty acid biosynthesis, was quantified in these strains to determine the regulatory influence of AozC on stress-responsive pathways.

8.q: We report for the first time a gene encoding calmodulin-dependent protein phosphatase from A. oryzae
